evals.prompt: I'm writing a Go CLI tool with Cobra and I want it to print a big bold 'MyApp' banner in cyan with a double border when it starts up. It should use the charmascii library, not shell out to the binary. Write the Go function.
evals.expected_output: A Go function using charmascii.DefaultOptions(), setting Font/Border/Color fields (e.g. doom or ansi_shadow font, double border, cyan color), calling charmascii.Generate, checking the error, and printing/returning result.Styled.
assertions: Calls charmascii.DefaultOptions() as the starting point
Sets a bold/impact font (e.g. doom, block, ansi_shadow) matching 'big bold' terminal request
Calls charmascii.Generate(text, opts) and checks the returned error
Prints or returns result.Styled (not result.Lines) for terminal display
Uses github.com/emmanuelgautier/charmascii import, no shelling out to the CLI binary (no exec.Command)

evals.expected_output: Uses charmascii.Generate, checks the error and returns an HTTP error on failure, and serializes result.Lines (not result.Styled) since Lines is always ANSI-free -- ideally without manually stripping ANSI or setting NoColor unnecessarily.
assertions: Calls charmascii.Generate and checks the returned error, writing an HTTP error response on failure
Serializes result.Lines (the ANSI-free field), not result.Styled, as the JSON 'lines' value
Does not manually strip ANSI codes with regex/string replace (Lines is already ANSI-free)
Sets the response Content-Type to application/json or uses json.NewEncoder/json.Marshal correctly
Uses the text query param as the input to Generate

evals.prompt: Write a small Go program using the charmascii package that prints a banner for every available font, so I can see what each one looks like.
evals.expected_output: Iterates charmascii.ListFonts() rather than a hardcoded font list, calls charmascii.Generate per font, checks errors per iteration (doesn't abort the whole loop on one bad font), and prints result.Styled or result.Lines.
assertions: Calls charmascii.ListFonts() to enumerate fonts rather than hardcoding a font name list
Loops over the returned font list, setting Options.Font per iteration
Calls charmascii.Generate per font and checks/handles the error without aborting the whole loop on one failure
Prints result.Styled or result.Lines for each font
Is a complete, compilable-looking main package (package main, func main, needed imports)
